prompt: I have a classifier with 15 categories and 200 labeled examples. Static few-shot demos only cover 3 categories at a time so accuracy is poor on the other 12. How do I make the demos adapt to each input?
expected_output: Uses KNNFewShot for dynamic per-input demo selection
assertions: Creates a dspy.Embedder with an embedding function (sentence-transformers or similar)
Sets k to a reasonable value (3-5) given 15 categories
Calls knn_optimizer.compile(program) to get a compiled module
Explains that each input automatically gets the most relevant demos at inference time

prompt: I want to use KNN to find similar examples from my training set for a custom pipeline. I do not want the full KNNFewShot optimizer, just the retrieval part.
expected_output: Uses dspy.KNN directly for nearest neighbor retrieval
assertions: Initializes with k, trainset, and vectorizer parameters
Calls knn(**kwargs) to retrieve similar examples
Shows that the result is a list of dspy.Example objects ranked by similarity
Explains that training examples must have .with_inputs() called

prompt: I want dynamic few-shot demos but I also want to use a metric to filter which demos are good. Can KNNFewShot do bootstrapping too?
assertions: Passes metric and max_bootstrapped_demos via **few_shot_bootstrap_args to KNNFewShot
Explains that KNNFewShot wraps BootstrapFewShot internally
Shows the two-stage process: KNN retrieves k neighbors, then BootstrapFewShot selects demos from those
Sets k higher than max_bootstrapped_demos + max_labeled_demos so BootstrapFewShot has examples to choose from
